_meta.title: Values Atlas — thin-spines candidate points (STAGING QUEUE)
_meta.purpose: Cited candidate civilization-at-epoch points proposed to densify the thinnest cells of the thread x epoch coverage grid. This is a PROPOSAL / staging queue for later human-reviewed ingestion.
_meta.DO_NOT_INGEST_AS_IS: This file is NOT public/data/atlas.json and must NOT be wired into the app. Every draftValue / draftDriver below is an INTERPRETIVE HYPOTHESIS that needs scholarly review before any point is written to the live dataset.
_meta.generatedFrom: public/data/atlas.json (67 points) parsed on branch feature/thin-spines-sourcing, off main.
_meta.schemaNote: Shape is deliberately different from AtlasPoint (top-level object, not a bare array; 'draftValues'/'draftDrivers' instead of 'values'/'drivers') so it cannot be mistaken for atlas.json. See lib/schema.ts for the real AtlasPoint shape used at ingestion time.
_meta.licensingPolicy: Path A (see LICENSING.md). Books are cited for NON-COPYRIGHTABLE FACTS + our own derivation (cite-don't-copy); CC-BY/CC0 datasets (D-PLACE, Pulotu, DRH) may be incorporated with attribution; Seshat is CC-BY-NC-SA and is therefore CITE-ONLY (no table/coded-value copying into the CC-BY-4.0 core).
_meta.epochAssignmentNote: Epoch is the NEAREST coarse bucket to a society's florescence, not an exact date. 'epochApproxDate' records the real archaeological/historical range for reviewers.
_meta.threadGap: Oceania/Austronesia, Japan, Persia, and Pharaonic Egypt currently have NO dedicated thread in lib/schema.ts and land in thread:'none'. Oceanic candidates below use thread:'none' for consistency with existing points (papua-hg, polynesia-med, arnhem-hg) and carry threadNote recommending a new 'oceania' thread be added to THREADS before ingestion.
_meta.tierOneReuse: Where the in-flight Tier 1 ingestion sources (DRH/SCCSR, Reba urbanization 3700BC-AD2000, Maddison Project, D-PLACE, Pulotu) already cover a candidate, it is listed in 'tierOneSources' so ingestion can reuse them rather than re-source.

candidates.title: Inca / Tawantinsuyu @ early-mod
candidates.rationale: The largest empire of the pre-Columbian Americas is entirely absent from the atlas - a glaring salience gap. Its reciprocity/redistribution economy (mit'a, ayni), road/storehouse logistics, and command agriculture are a distinctive values configuration unlike any Old-World state already covered.

draftValues.note: NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: ayllu reciprocity + state redistribution
NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: census/quipu administration, decimal labor org
NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: divine Sapa Inca, nested elite ranks
NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: state feasting/chicha as political reciprocity

candidates.reviewFlag: NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive. Values/drivers are hypotheses pending scholarly check.

citations.title: The Incas

candidates.title: Caral / Norte Chico @ bronze
candidates.rationale: Fills the EMPTY americas/bronze cell. The earliest urban, monumental society in the Americas - pre-ceramic, apparently without warfare/fortifications - is a powerful deep-time data point on monumentality preceding the state.

draftValues.note: NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: corporate labor on platform mounds
NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: temple/plaza ceremonial focus
NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: no fortifications/weapons reported

citations.title: Dating the Late Archaic occupation of the Norte Chico region in Peru
Dating Caral, a Preceramic Site in the Supe Valley on the Central Coast of Peru

candidates.title: Tiwanaku @ medieval
candidates.rationale: Fills the EMPTY americas/medieval cell. High-altitude raised-field (suka kollu) agriculture + a religious/colonial hinterland model; a non-militaristic-looking Andean state contrasting with Wari.

draftValues.note: NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: corporate raised-field labor
NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: Akapana/Kalasasaya ceremonial core
NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive

citations.title: The Tiwanaku: Portrait of an Andean Civilization

candidates.title: Cahokia / Mississippian @ medieval
candidates.rationale: Fills the EMPTY americas/medieval cell and adds North America north of Mexico (currently only frontier/modern USA). Largest pre-Columbian city north of Mexico; mound monumentality, feasting, and evidence of sacrifice.

draftValues.note: NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: chiefly elites, Mound 72 burials
NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: plaza/mound cosmology
NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: mass feasting as integration

candidates.reviewFlag: NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive. Degree of centralization (chiefdom vs. state) is contested.

citations.title: Cahokia: Ancient America's Great City on the Mississippi

candidates.title: Yamnaya / Pontic-Caspian pastoralists @ bronze
candidates.rationale: Fills the EMPTY steppe/bronze cell on the THINNEST spine (steppe has only 3 points). Wagon+horse mobile pastoralism and kurgan elite/patron-client institutions - the deep root of later steppe value-complexes (honor, raiding, hospitality).

draftValues.note: NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: warrior prestige, kurgan display
NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: mounted mobility, dispersed herding households
NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: guest-host / patron-client bonds
NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: raiding economy

candidates.reviewFlag: NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive. Values inferred from mortuary archaeology + comparative IE reconstruction.

citations.title: The Horse, the Wheel, and Language: How Bronze-Age Riders from the Eurasian Steppes Shaped the Modern World

candidates.title: Lapita cultural complex @ iron
candidates.threadNote: RECOMMEND adding 'oceania' thread to THREADS; currently lands in thread:'none' like papua-hg/polynesia-med/arnhem-hg.
candidates.rationale: Opens up the badly under-covered Oceania/Austronesia cluster (only 3 points exist). The Lapita expansion is the founding maritime-colonization horizon for the Pacific; values of exploration, kin-based voyaging, and long-distance exchange.

draftValues.note: NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: deliberate open-ocean exploration/colonization
NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: founder-colony cooperation
NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: exchange-network reciprocity (obsidian, pottery)

citations.title: The Lapita Peoples: Ancestors of the Oceanic World
On the Road of the Winds: An Archaeological History of the Pacific Islands before European Contact (rev. ed.)

candidates.title: Hawaiian archaic states @ early-mod
candidates.rationale: A textbook case of primary state formation / 'divine kingship' arising in isolation - kapu system, mana, heiau temple cult, irrigated and dryland intensification. Strong, well-licensed Austronesian scholarship.

draftValues.note: NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: ali'i/maka'ainana ranking, kapu
NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: heiau cult, divine chiefship
NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: mana competition among chiefs

citations.title: How Chiefs Became Kings: Divine Kingship and the Rise of Archaic States in Ancient Hawai'i

candidates.title: Kush / Meroe @ axial
candidates.rationale: Fills the EMPTY africa/axial cell. A literate (Meroitic script), iron-working Nile state that ruled Egypt (25th Dynasty) and later flourished independently - counters the 'Africa = non-literate' bias and adds an indigenous sub-Saharan script.

draftValues.note: NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: Amun/Apedemak cult, pyramid burials
NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: sacral kingship, candaces
NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: development of Meroitic writing

citations.title: The Kingdom of Kush: The Napatan and Meroitic Empires
The Nubian Past: An Archaeology of the Sudan

candidates.title: Kerma (Nubia) @ bronze
candidates.rationale: Fills the EMPTY africa/bronze cell. Sub-Saharan Africa's earliest urban state, rival to Middle Kingdom Egypt - monumental deffufa, royal tumuli with retainer sacrifice, deep-time African complexity.

draftValues.note: NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: huge royal tumuli + retainer sacrifice
NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: deffufa temple monumentality
NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: militarized rivalry with Egypt

citations.title: The Nubian Past: An Archaeology of the Sudan

candidates.title: Monte Alban / Zapotec @ axial
candidates.rationale: Fills the EMPTY americas/axial cell. A hilltop disembedded capital with one of the earliest Mesoamerican writing/calendar systems and conquest-slab propaganda - early state formation + literacy in the New World.

draftValues.note: NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: emergent state elites
NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: 'danzantes'/conquest slabs
NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: early glyphic writing + calendar

citations.title: Zapotec Civilization: How Urban Society Evolved in Mexico's Oaxaca Valley

candidates.title: Olmec (San Lorenzo / La Venta) @ iron
candidates.rationale: Fills the EMPTY americas/iron cell. Mesoamerica's 'mother culture' - colossal heads, early ritual complexity and long-distance jade/obsidian exchange that seeds later Maya/Zapotec.

draftValues.note: NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: colossal-head ruler portraits
NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: were-jaguar/rain cosmology

citations.title: The Olmecs: America's First Civilization

candidates.title: Classic Maya (Tikal) @ imperial
candidates.rationale: Americas/imperial holds only Teotihuacan. Classic Maya add a fully literate, astronomically sophisticated, politically fragmented civilization (divine kingship + endemic inter-polity war) - a distinct values mix (honor/prestige war + literacy + piety).

draftValues.note: NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: prestige warfare, captive-taking
NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: k'uhul ajaw divine kingship, bloodletting
NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: Long Count, astronomy, full writing

citations.title: Chronicle of the Maya Kings and Queens (2nd ed.)
The Ancient Maya (6th ed.)

candidates.title: Chavin de Huantar @ iron
candidates.rationale: Reinforces the EMPTY americas/iron cell with an Andean pan-regional religious/oracle center - a 'cult horizon' that integrated diverse peoples through ritual rather than conquest.

draftValues.note: NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: oracle/pilgrimage cult, Lanzon
NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: emergent religious elite

citations.title: Chavin and the Origins of Andean Civilization

candidates.title: Ancestral Puebloan / Chaco Canyon @ medieval
candidates.rationale: Adds a Southwest US data point to the empty americas/medieval cell: great-house monumentality + road network in an arid landscape, with a debated egalitarian-vs-hierarchical social order (rich contestable reading).

draftValues.note: NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: great kivas, ritual landscape
NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: communal labor, redistribution
NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: CONTESTED degree of elite control

candidates.reviewFlag: NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive. Hierarchy strongly contested - flag contestNote at ingestion.

citations.title: Ancient Peoples of the American Southwest (2nd ed.)

candidates.title: Haudenosaunee (Iroquois Confederacy) @ early-mod
candidates.epochApproxDate: Great Law formation c. 1450-1600; contact era 1500-1700
candidates.rationale: A non-state confederal polity with consensus governance, clan-matron authority and the Great Law of Peace - a rare egalitarian/individual-autonomy + collective-kinship configuration, and ethnographically very well documented (D-PLACE/SCCS).

draftValues.note: NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: consensus council, dispersed authority
NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: matrilineal clan/longhouse
NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: strong personal autonomy norm
NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: Great Law of Peace, condolence

citations.title: The Ordeal of the Longhouse: The Peoples of the Iroquois League in the Era of European Colonization
The Great Law and the Longhouse: A Political History of the Iroquois Confederacy

candidates.title: Songhai Empire @ early-mod
candidates.rationale: Africa/early-mod currently holds only Igbo. Songhai adds the largest West African empire - Timbuktu scholarship/literacy, codified administration, trans-Saharan trade - directly served by the verified Gomez (2018) synthesis.

draftValues.note: NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: Islamic statecraft, ulama
NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: provincial administration under Askia Muhammad
NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: Timbuktu manuscript scholarship

citations.title: African Dominion: A New History of Empire in Early and Medieval West Africa
Timbuktu and the Songhay Empire

candidates.title: Ghana Empire (Wagadu) @ medieval
candidates.rationale: Africa/medieval holds only Mali. Ghana adds the earlier trans-Saharan gold-salt state and dual-town (royal/Muslim-merchant) urban model, deepening West African time-depth before Mali.

draftValues.note: NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: sacral kingship, court ritual
NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: merchant-protection, dual-town tolerance

citations.title: African Dominion: A New History of Empire in Early and Medieval West Africa
Ancient Ghana and Mali

candidates.title: Great Zimbabwe @ medieval
candidates.rationale: Iconic dry-stone monumental state linking interior cattle/gold wealth to the Indian Ocean trade via the Swahili coast; also corrects a long colonial-era denial of indigenous African authorship (good contested/altReading material).

draftValues.note: NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: elite enclosure, cattle wealth
NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: ritual use of the Hill Complex

citations.title: The Zimbabwe Culture: Origins and Decline of Southern Zambezian States

candidates.title: Swahili coast (Kilwa) @ medieval
candidates.rationale: A cosmopolitan, Islamic, mercantile African urban network on the Indian Ocean - commerce, maritime exchange and a hybrid Afro-Arab-Persian culture. Complements Great Zimbabwe (its trade partner).

draftValues.note: NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: cosmopolitan merchant hospitality
NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: Islam, stone mosques
NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: merchant enterprise

citations.title: The Swahili: The Social Landscape of a Mercantile Society

candidates.title: Kingdom of Kongo @ early-mod
candidates.rationale: A Central African kingdom that adopted Christianity on its own terms and engaged Atlantic diplomacy/trade - a powerful case of African agency under intensifying colonial/slave-trade pressure (strong cost/altReading dimension).

candidates.rationale: Extends the Aksum thread forward: a long-lived Christian African state with rock-hewn church monumentality and a distinctive sacral monarchy/honor culture.

candidates.title: Wari Empire @ medieval
candidates.rationale: The more militaristic/administrative Andean counterpart to Tiwanaku (orthogonal compounds, colonies, road precursors). Pairs with Tiwanaku to contrast two Middle Horizon state strategies.

candidates.title: Toltec (Tula) @ medieval
candidates.rationale: Bridges Teotihuacan (imperial) and Mexica (early-mod) in central Mexico; the Toltec became the legitimating 'classical' reference for later Mesoamerican dynasties (militarism + craft prestige).

draftValues.note: NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: warrior cult (Atlantean columns)

candidates.title: Saka / Pazyryk nomads @ axial
candidates.rationale: Fills the EMPTY steppe/axial cell. Frozen-tomb (kurgan) preservation gives unusually direct evidence of dress, tattoo, horse gear and feasting - rich material for honor/individualism + art/dress aspects. (Related to but east of the iron-age Scythians already in the atlas.)

draftValues.note: NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: warrior burials, weapon grave goods
NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: personal adornment/tattoo, mounted autonomy

candidates.title: Gokturk Khaganate (Orkhon) @ medieval
candidates.rationale: Steppe/medieval holds only the Mongols. The Gokturks add the FIRST indigenous Turkic writing (Orkhon runiform inscriptions) - a literate steppe empire, countering the 'nomads = non-literate' assumption and giving a script aspect.

draftValues.note: NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: heroic ethos in Orkhon inscriptions

candidates.title: Dzungar / Kazakh Khanate @ early-mod
candidates.rationale: Fills the EMPTY steppe/early-mod cell. The last great independent steppe powers in the gunpowder age - their destruction by the Qing marks the historical 'closing' of the steppe frontier (strong colonial_pressure / cost narrative).

draftValues.note: NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: clan honor, mobile warfare

candidates.rationale: A Polynesian maritime 'empire' integrating islands across vast ocean distances via tribute and the langi monumental tombs - distinct from the Society Islands point already present (polynesia-med). Pulotu (CC-BY) directly supplies Tongan data.

candidates.title: Maori (Aotearoa) @ early-mod
candidates.epochApproxDate: Settlement c. 1250-1300; classic Maori society to 1769 contact
candidates.rationale: The southernmost Polynesian colonization, with a distinctive kin/honor complex (mana, utu, tapu, whakapapa) and well-documented ethnography (D-PLACE/SCCS) and CC-BY Pulotu coverage.

candidates.title: Rapa Nui (Easter Island) @ medieval
candidates.rationale: The most isolated human society on Earth, famous for moai monumentality and a fiercely contested 'collapse' narrative - an ideal point for contested/altReading treatment (ecocide vs. resilience-and-genocide debates).

draftValues.note: NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: ancestor moai cult

candidates.title: Jomon affluent foragers (Sannai-Maruyama) @ hunter
candidates.threadNote: Japan currently lands in thread:'none' (cf. meiji-japan, tokugawa-em).
candidates.rationale: The hunter cell is well stocked with MOBILE, egalitarian foragers (San, Hadza, Inuit, Yanomami). Jomon adds the contrasting 'complex/affluent sedentary forager' case - permanent villages, storage, pottery and ritual WITHOUT agriculture - widening the deep-time value space.

draftValues.note: NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: sedentary village cooperation
NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: dogu figurines, stone circles
NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: limited but debated status differences

candidates.reviewFlag: NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive. Degree of social complexity/inequality is actively debated.

citations.title: Ancient Jomon of Japan

candidates.title: Kuk Swamp / New Guinea Highlands agriculture @ early-agr
candidates.rationale: early-agr is dominated by Old-World/Andean cases. Kuk is one of the FEW independent global centres of plant domestication (taro, banana) and produced big-man reciprocity rather than hereditary hierarchy - a key non-state agricultural value-path. Verified DOI.

draftValues.note: NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: achieved (big-man) rather than ascribed status
NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: exchange/obligation networks
NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive: competitive gift/feasting (moka-type, later analogues)

candidates.reviewFlag: NEEDS REVIEW - interpretive. Value reconstruction blends archaeology with later Highlands ethnography - flag the inference.

citations.title: Origins of Agriculture at Kuk Swamp in the Highlands of New Guinea
